actionLibraryList.title: Mat Scapula Isolation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your back with a neutral spine and pelvis. Place your feet hip-width apart on the mat. Extend your arms straight toward the ceiling with palms facing inward. Keep the shoulder blades neutral.
2.Inhale, protract the shoulder blades, creating a stretch between them.
3.Exhale, return the shoulder blades to neutral, extending and stretching the posterior shoulder girdle.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770688675769.jpg","context":"1.Keep the lower back stable, pressing the sacrum into the mat.\n2.Keep the lower limbs firmly planted and still."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to return the shoulder blades to neutral.
Inhale to protract and stretch between the shoulder blades.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid over-retracting the shoulder blades during the movement.
2.Keep the spine stable; avoid any spinal movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Open Book
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your side in a neutral spine position. Relax the chest, align the pelvis and legs in a straight line with heels together. Extend the top arm forward at chest height. Engage the core.
2.Inhale, rotate the thoracic spine backward while opening the top arm horizontally toward the floor, keeping the pelvis and lower body stable.
3.Exhale, rotate the thoracic spine back to the starting position, bringing the arm back to the initial placement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770689393075.jpg","context":"1.Let the shoulders relax and sink naturally.\n2.Keep the pelvis vertical to the floor."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale to rotate the thoracic spine and open the arm.
Exhale to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the pelvis stable; avoid tilting or rocking forward/backward.
2.Keep the shoulders relaxed; avoid shrugging.
3.Maintain control of the arm; avoid swinging or using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Lying Straight Down
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale, brace your core, keep the arms straight, and drive the movement from the back as you press both arms down along your sides toward the outer thighs.
2.As the arms press down, keep the pelvis and spine stable. Maintain the tabletop position—do not let the legs shift or rock with the arm movement.
3.Inhale and, resisting the cable tension, slowly return the arms to the start with control, maintaining constant tension.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770626631633.jpg","context":"1.Bend the knees and hips to lift both legs into a tabletop position, with the knees stacked directly over the hips.\n2.Gently press the lower back into the mat and keep the pelvis stable.\n3.Relax the shoulders and let them drop away from the ears."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale as you press the arms straight down.
Inhale as you slowly return to the start.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the arms, shoulders, back, and core.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Do not let the legs move or pull the knees toward the chest.
2.Keep the lower back in contact with the mat and avoid rib flare.
3.Keep the elbows straight—do not bend the arms.
4.Do not shrug the shoulders or grip with the wrists.

actionLibraryList.title: Lying Arm Circles
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Open the arms out to the sides, lower them close to the floor, then press down. From near the hips, control the arms inward and upward, drawing circles along the sides of the body, and finish with the arms returning to a position above the chest.
3.Keep the arms below shoulder level throughout the movement.
4.Maintain a stable tabletop position with the legs, keeping the pelvis and spine steady the entire time.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770626713742.jpg","context":"1.Keep the knees stacked directly over the hips, maintaining a stable tabletop position.\n2.Gently press the lower back into the mat and keep the pelvis stable.\n3.Relax the shoulders and draw them down away from the ears."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the back, core, and arms.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the ribs from flaring and avoid arching the lower back.
2.Avoid shrugging the shoulders.
3.Keep the wrists neutral—don’t flex them.
4.Don’t let the knees drift toward the chest.

actionLibraryList.title: Thigh Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Exhale. Engage the core to maintain stability and, using the knees as the pivot, slowly lean the body backward with control.
2.Inhale. Keep the core engaged and, following the arms pulling the rope, slowly return the body to a neutral position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770631838108.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders down.\n2.Maintain a stable pelvis."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to lean back.
Inhale to return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the pelvis stable; avoid sagging or arching the lower back.
2.Perform the movement with control; avoid quickly releasing the rope.

actionLibraryList.title: Swan
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie prone with your arms naturally extended, forehead slightly lowered, spine elongated, and legs straight in contact with the mat. Inhale as you stabilize the shoulder blades downward, gently press the arms into the mat, and sequentially extend the upper back, opening the chest forward and upward while keeping the head and neck aligned with the spine.
2.Exhale as you engage the core and control the spine rolling down segment by segment, returning to the prone starting position. Repeat the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770687890084.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders down and stable.\n2.Keep the legs stable and in contact with the mat."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Inhale to extend the upper back and open the chest.
Exhale to engage the core and control the spine as it rolls down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid pressing down with your arms and neglecting back engagement.
2.Avoid shrugging the shoulders or excessively tilting the neck backward.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Mermaid
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Sit with the left leg bent, inner knee touching the mat, and the right leg bent with the thigh externally rotated, naturally folded. Keep the pelvis stable, sitting firmly on both sit bones. Extend the arms out to the sides at shoulder height to prepare.
2.Exhale, lead the torso upward and sideways with the left arm, creating a side bend and an arched stretch. Support the arm naturally on the mat, ensuring both sit bones stay grounded.
3.Hold at the top of the side bend for 3 seconds, breathing naturally while continuing to lengthen both sides of the body and the intercostal space.
4.Inhale, engage the side waist and core, articulate the torso back to upright sitting. Repeat the movement and switch sides.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770689607617.jpg","context":"1.Let the shoulders relax and sink naturally.\n2.Keep the pelvis neutral and stable."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to side bend and extend.
Inhale to return to upright.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid sagging or arching the lower back.
2.Keep the pelvis stable; avoid tilting or rotating.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Open Book Arm Circle
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie on your side with a neutral spine. Relax the chest, align the pelvis and legs in a straight line with heels together. Extend the top arm forward at chest height and engage the core. Inhale to prepare.
2.Exhale, move the top arm in a large circular motion overhead and behind, as far as comfortable.
3.Inhale as the arm swings back toward the front of the hip, returning to the starting position. Continue exhaling as you repeat the circular motion.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770689405356.jpg","context":"1.Let the shoulders relax and sink naturally.\n2.Keep the pelvis vertical to the floor."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale to open and circle the arm.
Inhale to return and recover to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the pelvis stable; avoid tilting or rocking forward/backward.
2.Keep the shoulders relaxed; avoid shrugging.
3.Maintain control of the arm; avoid swinging or using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Mat Cat & Cow
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Begin in a quadruped position with hands under the shoulders and knees under the hips. Keep the spine neutral, pelvis stable, and core engaged. Inhale to prepare.
2.Exhale, draw the abdomen inward and upward, tilt the pelvis posteriorly, and sequentially roll the spine from the tailbone, rounding the back to the highest point into Cat Pose.
3.Inhale, return the torso to a neutral position, then sequentially extend the spine from the tailbone to the crown of the head, opening the chest forward into Cow Pose.
4.Exhale, return to neutral and roll again. Flow smoothly between flexion and extension with the breath, maintaining stable shoulder blades and core engagement throughout.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770690119784.jpg","context":"1.Keep the spine and pelvis stable; avoid collapsing the lower back.\n2.Relax the shoulders and neck, letting them drop down naturally."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate:
Exhale, engage the abdominals, and flex the spine segment by segment.
Inhale, lengthen and open the spine.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid collapsing the lower back or over-compressing the lumbar spine, maintaining neutral control.
2.Keep the shoulders relaxed and avoid compensating with the arms.
3.Avoid moving only the head or pelvis; ensure the spine moves segment by segment.

actionLibraryList.title: Glutes Stretch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lightly engage the core, keep the pelvis neutral and stable, lower the shoulder blades, and relax the neck. Bring one leg forward, bending at the hip and knee, and extend the other leg back with the thigh on the mat. Keep the hips facing forward without tilting.
2.Inhale to lengthen the spine and level the pelvis. Exhale as you hinge forward from the hips, resting the forearms or forehead lightly on the mat. Maintain the back hip extended forward without collapsing the lower back, feeling a stretch through the hips and glutes. Breathe evenly while holding the stretch.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770691212351.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine and stable pelvis, avoiding any tilting."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe evenly while in the stretch.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a stretch in the hips.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.
2.Maintain a neutral spine and avoid tilting the pelvis.

actionLibraryList.title: Child Pose
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start in a kneeling position with the knees hip-width apart. Press the tops of the feet into the mat, sit the hips back toward the heels, and keep the pelvis stable.
2.Lengthen the spine, then hinge forward from the hips. Gently rest the forehead on the mat, extending the arms forward or placing them alongside the body to allow a gentle stretch through the lats and lower back. Keep the hips as close to the heels as possible and breathe evenly throughout the stretch.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1770690951913.jpg","context":"1.Keep the shoulders relaxed and down.\n2.Maintain a neutral spine with a stable pelvis."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel a stretch along the spine.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection:
1.Avoid shrugging the shoulders.
2.Avoid arching the lower back.
